text: Good API design makes your application easier to use and maintain. When building REST APIs, you should focus on clear and consistent endpoints. Use nouns, not verbs, for your resource URLs, like `/users` instead of `/getUsers`. Choose meaningful HTTP status codes to inform clients about success or errors, such as `200` for success, `201` for created, or `404` for not found. Always provide clear documentation explaining each endpoint and its parameters. Additionally, implement versioning, for example by using `/v1/` in your URL path. This ensures that when you make changes, existing users can continue using the old version while you develop new features.
